methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe consists of 7 private companies in this space, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public companies serve as direct benchmarks for valuation.

Traditional peer-median methodology is not applicable here. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only 4 same-sector peers overall, and filtering for seed-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies yields fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. A broad sector median would introduce significant noise and obscure rather than clarify valuation drivers specific to early-stage autonomous vehicle development.

Without sufficient peer clustering, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range at this time. Meaningful valuation work on Airship Industries would require either: (1) expansion of data on the identified peer set to establish funding rounds, valuations, and development milestones; (2) application of cost-of-capital or discounted cash flow methods if financial projections are available; or (3) benchmarking against earlier funding rounds or comparable seed investments in adjacent hardware-intensive autonomous systems. Key uncertainties center on technology differentiation, capital intensity through commercialization, and regulatory pathway clarity—factors that typically drive wide valuation ranges in pre-revenue frontier-tech companies.
